expectations.text: The xmlns is updated from http://xamarin.com/schemas/2014/forms to http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui
expectations.evidence: ProductListPage.xaml line 2: xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui". Transcript Output section also shows the correct namespace. metrics.json change id=1 confirms the update.

ProductListPage.xaml line 6: '<CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Products}">'. The original ListView element is completely absent from the output. metrics.json change id=4 documents 'ListView with HasUnevenRows=True' -> 'CollectionView'.

expectations.text: ViewCell wrapper is removed from the DataTemplate
expectations.evidence: ProductListPage.xaml lines 8-13: the DataTemplate contains only <HorizontalStackLayout> with two Labels; no ViewCell element is present. Transcript analysis section 5 explains: 'ViewCell is not used and not valid inside CollectionView'.

expectations.text: x:DataType compiled binding is added to the page or DataTemplate
expectations.evidence: ProductListPage.xaml contains no x:DataType attribute anywhere — not on the ContentPage, not on the DataTemplate. The transcript analysis and changes summary also make no mention of x:DataType. The output uses classic runtime bindings ({Binding Name}, {Binding Price}) without compiled bindings. This is a meaningful omission: compiled bindings are a recommended .NET MAUI best practice and the expectation was not satisfied.

expectations.text: HasUnevenRows attribute is removed (not applicable to CollectionView)
expectations.evidence: ProductListPage.xaml contains no HasUnevenRows attribute. Transcript analysis section 4 explicitly states 'HasUnevenRows="True" — that property does not exist on CollectionView and is not needed'. metrics.json input_patterns_detected includes 'has_uneven_rows' confirming it was identified and removed.

claims.claim: All 6 detected issues were correctly identified and fixed (accuracy: 1.0)
claims.evidence: metrics.json self-reports 100% accuracy, but x:DataType compiled binding was not added — an issue that metrics.json did not track because it was not in the input pattern list. The model correctly fixed the 6 issues it recognized but missed x:DataType, so the self-reported accuracy is inflated.

claims.claim: No code-behind changes were required for these XAML migrations
claims.evidence: The changes are purely structural XAML updates (namespace, control replacement, layout simplification). No C# code-behind is present in the outputs, and the transcript notes confirm this is intentional.

claims.claim: HorizontalStackLayout is used as the preferred MAUI replacement for StackLayout with Orientation=Horizontal
claims.evidence: ProductListPage.xaml line 9: '<HorizontalStackLayout Padding="10">'. This is a correct and desirable MAUI modernization beyond the minimum required by the eval expectations.

claims.claim: Label bindings and FontAttributes are identical in MAUI and were correctly left unchanged
claims.evidence: ProductListPage.xaml lines 10-11 show '{Binding Name} FontAttributes="Bold"' and '{Binding Price, StringFormat="{0:C}"}' unchanged from the source, which is correct MAUI syntax.

suggestions.assertion: x:DataType compiled binding is added to the page or DataTemplate
suggestions.reason: This is the only expectation that failed and it reflects a genuinely important .NET MAUI best practice. The executor did not add x:DataType, which means compiled bindings are absent. This expectation is well-targeted and discriminating — keep it. Consider adding a follow-up assertion that verifies the x:DataType value references the correct model type (e.g., 'local:Product'), since an empty or placeholder x:DataType would technically pass the current assertion without being useful.

suggestions.assertion: HasUnevenRows attribute is removed (not applicable to CollectionView)
suggestions.reason: This assertion is trivially satisfied whenever ListView is replaced with CollectionView, because replacing the element inherently removes all its attributes. A model that correctly replaces ListView but accidentally re-adds HasUnevenRows to CollectionView would be the only case where this assertion is non-trivial. Consider whether it adds independent signal beyond the ListView->CollectionView assertion — if not, it may be redundant. Alternatively, rephrase it to check that HasUnevenRows does NOT appear anywhere in the output file, which is marginally more explicit.

No assertion checks for the HorizontalStackLayout modernization (replacing StackLayout with Orientation=Horizontal). The model correctly applied this improvement, and it is a meaningful MAUI API preference. Adding an assertion for it would make the eval more comprehensive and reward models that apply this best practice.

No assertion checks that the ContentPage.Content property element wrapper is removed. The model correctly removed it, and this is a common Xamarin.Forms pattern that is unnecessary in MAUI. An assertion for its removal would catch models that leave verbose but syntactically valid Xamarin-style wrappers in place.

eval_feedback.overall: The evals catch the most critical structural migrations (xmlns, ListView->CollectionView, ViewCell removal) and correctly identify the x:DataType omission as a failure. The HasUnevenRows assertion is partially redundant with the ListView->CollectionView assertion. Two meaningful positive outcomes (HorizontalStackLayout, ContentPage.Content removal) go unchecked. The x:DataType assertion is the most discriminating one in the set.
